description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly gray coloration and subtle hints of brown and white mineral deposits. Its surface is rough and uneven, featuring natural cracks, chips, and a weathered texture typical of stones found in outdoor environments. The rock has a somewhat flattened profile on one side, while the opposite side is more rounded and jagged. The coloration is mottled, with darker and lighter patches blending together, suggesting a composition of granite or similar hard stone. There are no visible markings, carvings, or artificial modifications on its surface. The rock appears solid and dense, suitable for use as a natural barrier or landscaping element in an urban setting.
description: A rough, gray, irregularly shaped rock with mottled brown and white mineral patches and a weathered, natural texture.
description_view_0: The rock appears broad and oval, with a rough, uneven surface and visible cracks along the edge.
description_view_1: Seen from the side, the rock looks much narrower and reveals its thickness and jagged profile.
description_view_2: This view shows a flatter, more rectangular face with mottled gray and brown coloration and a prominent edge.
description_view_3: The rock is seen edge-on, highlighting its thinness and the rough, slightly curved surface.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
